options.text: Petroleum having a flashpoint of 60 Degrees C or above
Petroleum having a flashpoint of 60 Degrees C or below
Petroleum having a flashpoint of 100 Degrees C or above
Petroleum having a flashpoint of 100 Degrees C or below

explanation: Non-volatile petroleum is defined as petroleum having a flash point of 60°C (140°F) or above, meaning it is less likely to form dangerous vapor concentrations under standard operational temperatures.
